india_trends.title: Smallcap stocks have already erased Iran war losses. Is a bluechip breakout next?
india_trends.summary: India’s smallcap index has recovered above pre-war levels following the US-Israel-Iran conflict, while the Nifty and midcaps still trade below their late-February peaks, highlighting a sharp market divergence. india_trends.title: Can oil prices above $90-100 over next few months pull Nifty 50 to 20,000 levels? What experts say
india_trends.summary: As the Indian stock markets have moved from earnings-driven to oil-driven trading in the near term, analysts expect higher-for-longer prices to drive another 8-10% correction in Sensex and Nifty 50.

india_trends.title: Top Gainers & Losers on April 15: RailTel Corp, Coforge, Anant Raj, Suzlon Energy, Hindustan Zinc among top gainers
india_trends.summary: On April 13, Indian markets surged as investors reacted positively to potential US-Iran negotiations. The Nifty and BSE Sensex gained 1.63% and 1.64%, respectively, while many stocks in technology and real estate sectors performed well, though a few faced declines.

india_trends.title: Indian rupee ends flat as importer dollar bids wipe boost from oil retreat
india_trends.summary: The Indian rupee closed nearly unchanged ​on Wednesday, wedged between ​comfort from a decline in crude oil prices and ​persistent dollar demand from local importers, including oil marketing companies.

india_trends.title: Semiconductor, railway infra stock jumps following Dalal Street rally
india_trends.summary: MIC Electronics shares rose 6% to ₹40.71, driven by strong market interest and recent order wins from Indian Railways. Despite a 48% decline over six months, the stock has surged 4,740% in five years, showcasing significant investor returns.

india_trends.title: Gold prices fall over 10% since US-Iran war. 5 reasons why safe-haven asset is under pressure
india_trends.summary: Gold prices fell on April 15 due to lower demand, dropping 0.23% to ₹1,54,464 per 10 grams. Internationally, spot gold also declined 0.6%. Analysts attribute the drop to rising interest rates, a stronger US dollar, and profit-taking among investors amid geopolitical tensions.
